title: claude_hooks multi-directory support
close_reason: Implemented: hooks_dir->hooks_dirs Vec, backward-compat deserializer, multi-dir evaluator enumerating all .sh files, deny-first across dirs, doctor updated, global init absolute path fix, merge unions dirs. All 60 tests pass.

title: [Epic] Global hooky configuration & configurable shims
description:
Source: design-global-config.md

Summary: Implement global hooky configuration system that allows users to define rules once and install shims once for all projects. Includes configurable shim list to support blocking any command (aws, gcloud, kubectl, etc), not just hardcoded commands.

Phases:
1. Add configurable shim list to config schema
2. Implement config hierarchy (global + local merging)
3. Implement global shims installation
4. Add enhanced CLI commands (config show, init --global)

Key files:
- src/hooky/config.rs (add ShimsConfig, merge logic)
- src/bin/hooky.rs (update install-shims, add new commands)
- design-global-config.md (design reference)
notes: Progress: All tests passing, running final validation

title: Add ShimsConfig to config schema
description:
Context: Phase 1 - Configurable shim list. See design-global-config.md section 2.

What to do:
- Add ShimsConfig struct to src/hooky/config.rs
- Add 'shims' field to Config struct with default
- Add default_shim_commands() function returning current hardcoded list
- Add serde derive for ShimsConfig
- Update Config::default() to include ShimsConfig::default()

Acceptance criteria:
- ShimsConfig struct exists with 'commands: Vec<String>' field
- Config struct has 'shims: ShimsConfig' field
- default_shim_commands() returns [git, rm, mv, curl, bash, sh]
- cargo build passes
- cargo test passes

Key files:
- src/hooky/config.rs (add structs)

title: Update documentation for global-first workflow
description:
Context: Phase 4 - Enhanced commands. Update all docs to reflect global-first approach.

What to do:
- Update README.md quickstart to use global workflow
- Update design-global-config.md to reflect global-first defaults
- Document: 'hooky install-shims' installs globally by default
- Document: 'hooky init' creates global config by default
- Document: use --local flag for project-specific setup
- Add examples showing typical workflow
- Add section on when to use local overrides

Acceptance criteria:
- README.md shows global-first quickstart
- design-global-config.md updated with new defaults
- Examples show: hooky init, hooky install-shims, add to PATH
- Clear guidance on when/how to use local overrides
- Documentation is consistent across all files

Key files:
- README.md (update quickstart)
- design-global-config.md (update defaults)
- Add examples/

title: Update install-shims to use configurable shim list
description:
Context: Phase 1 - Configurable shim list. Depends on ShimsConfig being added to config schema.

What to do:
- Update install_shims() to accept &[String] for commands instead of using SHIM_COMMANDS constant
- Update install_shims_command() to load config and extract shims.commands
- Pass config.shims.commands to install_shims()
- Update run_program() similarly (it calls install_shims internally)
- Keep SHIM_COMMANDS constant as fallback for now

Acceptance criteria:
- install_shims() signature takes commands: &[String] parameter
- install-shims command reads config and uses configured shim list
- hooky run reads config and uses configured shim list for auto-install
- If config has 'shims.commands: [git, aws]', only git and aws shims are created
- cargo test passes
- Manual test: create .hooky.yml with custom shim list, run install-shims, verify correct shims created

Key files:
- src/bin/hooky.rs (update install_shims functions)

title: Add global config loading and merging logic
description:
Context: Phase 2 - Config hierarchy. Enables ~/.hooky/config.yml support with merging.

What to do:
- Add dirs crate dependency for home_dir()
- Add Config::load_global() -> Result<Option<Config>> (loads ~/.hooky/config.yml)
- Add Config::load_local() -> Result<Option<Config>> (loads ./.hooky.yml)
- Add Config::load_merged() -> Result<Config> (merges global + local)
- Add MergeStrategy enum (Extend, Replace, Prepend)
- Add merge_configs(global, local) function
- Add merge_engine(global, local) function for each engine type
- Local config takes precedence: local mode overrides global, local rules evaluated first

Acceptance criteria:
- Config::load_global() loads from ~/.hooky/config.yml if it exists
- Config::load_merged() merges global and local configs correctly
- MergeStrategy enum works for rules merging
- Local rules are evaluated before global rules (order matters!)
- cargo test passes
- Unit tests for merging: test_merge_extends_rules, test_merge_replaces_rules, test_local_overrides_global_mode

Key files:
- src/hooky/config.rs (add merge logic)
- Cargo.toml (add dirs = "5.0")
notes: Progress: Implementation complete - all acceptance criteria met. Running final verification.

title: Update CLI commands to use merged config
description:
Context: Phase 2 - Config hierarchy. Update all commands to load merged config instead of single config file.

What to do:
- Update run_program() to use Config::load_merged()
- Update run_check_shell() to use Config::load_merged()
- Update run_check_argv() to use Config::load_merged()
- Update run_doctor() to use Config::load_merged()
- Update install_shims_command() to use Config::load_merged()
- Ensure config path parameter still works (overrides auto-discovery)

Acceptance criteria:
- All commands load both global and local configs when no --config specified
- Commands respect --config flag when provided (single file mode)
- hooky run uses merged config for rule evaluation
- hooky check-shell and check-argv use merged config
- Manual test: create ~/.hooky/config.yml with block rule, verify it applies in any project directory
- cargo test passes

Key files:
- src/bin/hooky.rs (update all command functions)

title: Make install-shims default to global (add --local flag)
description:
Context: Phase 3 - Global shims. Default to installing shims globally, with --local opt-in for project-specific.

What to do:
- Add 'local: bool' field to InstallShims command in CLI
- Update install_shims_command() to default to ~/.hooky/shims
- When --local: install to ./.hooky/shims instead
- Use home_dir() from dirs crate
- Update output message to indicate global vs local installation
- Always print suggestion to add ~/.hooky/shims to PATH after global install (unless already in PATH)

Acceptance criteria:
- 'hooky install-shims' (no flag) installs to ~/.hooky/shims (global by default)
- 'hooky install-shims --local' installs to ./.hooky/shims
- Output clearly shows installation location
- Global install prints: 'Add to your shell profile: export PATH="$HOME/.hooky/shims:$PATH"'
- Check if PATH already contains ~/.hooky/shims before suggesting
- cargo test passes
- Manual test: run without flag, verify shims in ~/.hooky/shims

Key files:
- src/bin/hooky.rs (add --local flag, update install_shims_command)

title: Update shim templates for global/local config discovery
description:
Context: Phase 3 - Global shims. Shims need to discover config from project dir first, then global dir.

What to do:
- Update build_command_shim_script() to add config discovery logic
- Update build_safe_shell_script() to add config discovery logic
- Logic: if .hooky.yml exists, use it; else if ~/.hooky/config.yml exists, use it; else use .hooky.yml (fallback)
- Update shim to use 'which -a <cmd> | grep -v .hooky/shims | head -n1' to find real binary
- This prevents shim from calling itself when installed globally

Acceptance criteria:
- Shim script checks for ./.hooky.yml first
- Shim script falls back to ~/.hooky/config.yml
- Shim script finds real binary even when ~/.hooky/shims is in PATH
- Manual test: install global shims, create project without .hooky.yml, verify global config is used
- Manual test: install global shims, create project with .hooky.yml, verify local config is used
- cargo test passes

Key files:
- src/bin/hooky.rs (update build_command_shim_script, build_safe_shell_script)

title: Make 'hooky init' default to global (add --local flag)
description:
Context: Phase 3 - Global config setup. Default to creating global config, with --local opt-in.

What to do:
- Add Init command to CLI with 'local: bool' flag
- Implement run_init() function
- By default: create ~/.hooky/ directory if not exists
- By default: create ~/.hooky/config.yml with template (example rules, shim list)
- By default: create ~/.hooky/hooks/ directory
- When --local: create ./.hooky.yml with template
- Template should include shims.commands list with common commands (git, rm, mv, curl, bash, sh, aws, gcloud, kubectl, terraform, docker)
- Template should include commented example rules for common scenarios
- Print next steps after creation

Acceptance criteria:
- 'hooky init' (no flag) creates ~/.hooky/config.yml with template
- 'hooky init --local' creates ./.hooky.yml with template
- Template includes shims.commands list with common cloud/infra tools
- Template includes example rules (commented out) for aws, terraform, kubectl, force-push, no-verify
- Output suggests running 'hooky install-shims' next (which also defaults to global)
- cargo test passes

Key files:
- src/bin/hooky.rs (add Init command, run_init function)

title: Update doctor to validate global config and shims
description:
Context: Phase 3 - Global shims. Doctor should check global config validity and PATH setup.

What to do:
- Update doctor::run() to check both global and local configs
- Add check: if ~/.hooky/config.yml exists, validate it
- Add check: if ~/.hooky/shims exists, check if it's in PATH
- Add check: if rules reference commands not in shims.commands, warn
- Add warning: 'Rule block-aws-cli blocks aws but no aws shim installed'
- Update DoctorReport to include global config status
- Print suggestions for fixing issues

Acceptance criteria:
- 'hooky doctor' validates both ~/.hooky/config.yml and ./.hooky.yml
- Doctor warns if ~/.hooky/shims exists but not in PATH
- Doctor warns if rules reference unshimmed commands
- Doctor output clearly shows global vs local config status
- cargo test passes
- Manual test: create global config with aws rule but no aws shim, verify warning

Key files:
- src/hooky/doctor.rs (add global config checks)

title: Add 'hooky config show' command
description:
Context: Phase 4 - Enhanced commands. Help users understand effective merged config.

What to do:
- Add Config subcommand with Show variant
- Implement run_config_show() function
- Load merged config using Config::load_merged()
- Pretty-print merged config as YAML
- Add annotations showing which parts came from global vs local
- Add --format flag (yaml|json)

Acceptance criteria:
- 'hooky config show' displays merged config as YAML
- 'hooky config show --format json' displays as JSON
- Output is valid YAML/JSON that could be used as a config file
- cargo test passes
- Manual test: create global and local configs, verify show displays merged result

Key files:
- src/bin/hooky.rs (add Config command, run_config_show)
